deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 6, Transcript 2 Turn 3, Transcript 3 Turn 8
deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no rationale is offered about active reasoning, retention, or skill development. The tutor enforces a Socratic approach throughout but never articulates to the student why this method supports learning.

deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 10, Transcript 2 Turn 10, Transcript 3 Turn 10
deductions.reason: In all three transcripts, after the student successfully builds a position from earlier confusion, the tutor moves forward with new prompts or refinements without pausing to ask the student to reflect on what changed in their thinking or what made the improved reasoning work.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: it never gives away answers, consistently prompts the student to derive distinctions (means vs. side effect, act consequentialism's core rule), builds on student progress turn by turn, and keeps responses concise and well-targeted. The main weaknesses are the absence of any explicit rationale for the tutoring approach (why guided discovery rather than direct answers) and a missed opportunity after student improvement to prompt reflection on what changed in their thinking.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is fully intact: the tutor never produces submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently lets the student generate key ideas before confirming them. 1.2 is fully intact: the tutor builds on each correct student step and diagnoses confusion before responding (e.g., asking the student to reconstruct Fuel/Blood from memory before clarifying). 1.3 loses 2 points under 1.3.A.a because in none of the three transcripts does the tutor explain why it is using a guided-discovery approach rather than simply providing answers—no mention of learning rationale, retention, or skill-building. 1.3 also loses 2 points under 1.3.C.a because after students successfully articulate improved positions (Turns 8–10 in each transcript), the tutor moves directly to the next refinement without prompting the student to reflect on what shifted in their reasoning. No deductions apply to dialogue quality or communication quality: the tutor stays on-task, keeps responses bite-sized, maintains a supportive tone, and avoids grading language.
